channel.title: Kyunolab Mystery Archive
channel.description: Legends, folklore, mysteries, and strange tales.

item.title: Vanishing Hitchhiker Urban Legend: The Passenger Who Disappears Before Arrival
item.description: The Vanishing Hitchhiker is a widely known urban legend about a driver who picks up a quiet passenger on a lonely road. The passenger disappears before the ride ends, or the driver later learns that the person died years earlier.

item.title: Polybius Arcade Game Legend: The Missing Cabinet, Rumor, and Internet Folklore
item.description: Polybius is an internet folklore story about a mysterious arcade game supposedly tested in the early 1980s. The legend says the cabinet caused strange physical or psychological effects, then disappeared.

item.title: Bennington Triangle: Disappearances, Mountain Roads, and a Vermont Place Legend
item.description: The Bennington Triangle is a regional place legend connected with disappearances around Glastenbury Mountain and the Bennington area of Vermont. The name groups several events into one mysterious landscape.

item.title: Oak Island Money Pit: Treasure, Tunnels, and the Mystery That Refuses to End
item.description: The Oak Island Money Pit is a famous Nova Scotia mystery about a supposed buried treasure site discovered in the late eighteenth century. Later stories describe platforms, flood tunnels, strange finds, and repeated digs.

item.title: Wild Hunt Folklore: Ghostly Riders, Storm Skies, and the Sound of a Passing Host
item.description: The Wild Hunt is a European folklore motif about a supernatural host, often heard or seen moving through the night sky, winter weather, or storm. It may appear as ghostly riders, hunters, hounds, or a procession of the dead.

item.title: Men in Black Legend: UFO Witnesses, Silent Visitors, and Modern Conspiracy Folklore
item.description: The Men in Black legend describes strange dark-suited visitors who allegedly appear after UFO sightings, ask questions, and warn witnesses to keep quiet. The figures are usually calm, intimidating, and slightly wrong.

item.title: Prometheus Fire Myth: Theft, Punishment, and the Gift That Changed Humanity
item.description: Prometheus is a Greek mythic figure remembered for stealing fire and giving it to humanity. Zeus punishes him for crossing divine limits, often by binding him while an eagle tears at him.

item.title: Phoenix Mythic Bird: Fire, Renewal, and the Creature That Returns From Ashes
item.description: The phoenix is a mythic bird associated with fire, death, and renewal. In many later versions, it dies in flame or on a nest and returns from ashes.

item.title: Shambhala Hidden Kingdom: Sacred Geography, Prophecy, and the Lost World People Seek
item.description: Shambhala is a hidden kingdom associated with Buddhist tradition, sacred geography, and prophecy. Later Western writing often transformed it into a lost-world or hidden-paradise idea.

item.title: Marfa Lights Mystery: Desert Glow, Witness Reports, and the Strange Nature of West Texas
item.description: The Marfa Lights are mysterious lights reported in the desert near Marfa, Texas. Witnesses describe glowing points that appear, move, split, fade, or hover in the distance.

item.title: Mount Olympus in Greek Myth: Home of the Gods and the Place Above the Human World
item.description: Mount Olympus is both a real mountain in Greece and the mythic home of the Olympian gods. In Greek tradition, it becomes the place above human affairs where divine power gathers.

item.title: Mjolnir: Thor Hammer, Protection Symbol, and the Mythic Object That Returns
item.description: Mjolnir is Thor hammer in Norse myth, a powerful weapon associated with thunder, protection, consecration, and divine force. It is famous for being hurled and returning.

item.title: Jack-o-Lantern Origin: Stingy Jack, Turnips, and the Halloween Light
item.description: The Jack-o-Lantern is a Halloween lantern custom often linked to the Irish story of Stingy Jack. Older versions involve carved turnips or root vegetables before pumpkins became the familiar North American form.

item.title: Kidney Theft Urban Legend: Bathtub Ice, Travel Fear, and the Organ Theft Rumor
item.description: The kidney theft urban legend usually describes a traveler who is drugged, wakes in a bathtub full of ice, and discovers that one or both kidneys have been removed. The story spread widely through fax, email, and word of mouth in the 1990s.

item.title: D.B. Cooper Hijacking: Ransom, Parachute, and the Unsolved Man in the Suit
item.description: D.B. Cooper is the name attached to the unknown man who hijacked Northwest Orient Flight 305 on November 24, 1971, received $200,000 in ransom, and parachuted from the aircraft into the night. His identity and fate remain unresolved.

item.title: Island of the Dolls: Xochimilco, Don Julian, and the Strange Place Legend
item.description: The Island of the Dolls is a strange place in the Xochimilco canals near Mexico City, known for trees and structures covered with old dolls. The common legend links the dolls to Don Julian Santana and the spirit of a drowned girl.

item.title: Tunguska Event: Siberian Explosion, Flattened Forest, and the Natural Mystery That Changed the Sky
item.description: The Tunguska Event was a massive explosion over Siberia on June 30, 1908. Modern scientific summaries generally interpret it as an asteroid or bolide airburst that flattened forest over a huge area without leaving a normal crater.

item.title: Narcissus and Echo: Reflection, Voice, and the Greek Myth of Unreachable Love
item.description: Narcissus and Echo is a Greek myth about two forms of unreachable love. Echo can only repeat the words of others, while Narcissus becomes trapped by his own reflection and cannot reach the image he desires.

item.title: Spear of Destiny: Holy Lance, Relic Tradition, and the Object That Claims History
item.description: The Spear of Destiny is a legendary name often attached to the Holy Lance, the spear associated with the piercing of Christ in Christian tradition. Several relic traditions exist, and later occult stories added claims of world-shaping power.

item.title: Cockatrice Folklore: Deadly Glance, Serpent-Rooster Form, and Medieval Fear
item.description: The cockatrice is a medieval fabulous creature often imagined as part rooster and part serpent. It was believed to kill through a deadly look, breath, touch, or poison, and it is closely related to basilisk traditions.
